summary:
Claim: Congenital rubella syndrome is linked to limb reduction defects and skin scarring due to impaired dermal development during embryogenesis.  
Evidence: - Rubella virus disrupts normal dermal patterning, leading to oligodactyly and radial ray deficiencies. - It induces cutaneous scarring from abnormal epidermal-dermal interactions in developing skin layers.  
Answer: C
